brief_justification: Chose A over the correct answer D — the agent correctly computed 99.9999875% but misidentified 'mathematically consistent' to mean 'compatible in principle' rather than 'yields the same number,' so the wrong answer was selected despite sound arithmetic; goes one level deeper by pre-emptively refuting D and invoking real-world factors, but never examines what 'mathematically consistent' actually demands of the two claims; addresses option D reactively but never pauses to interrogate whether its own interpretation of 'consistent' is the one the question intends; verifies the arithmetic step and checks that 99.9999875% > 99.99%, but does not test the boundary condition of what would make the claims inconsistent; acknowledges real-world factors could explain the gap but presents A as unambiguously correct without flagging the interpretive ambiguity in 'consistent'; names D and explains its objection, but does not explore the reading under which D's framing is valid; every arithmetic step is explicitly shown and a third party could reproduce the full derivation chain.
